Usage: belz cache [flags] [uuid...]

Invalidate server-side cache entries for the active environment via
POST /rest/api/cache/invalidate.

Pass one or more UUIDs to invalidate specific entries, or omit UUIDs to
invalidate the entire cache for the given --type. Wiping the entire cache
requires --yes (or interactive confirmation).

Args:
  [uuid...]           One or more cache entry UUIDs. Omit to clear all.

Flags:
  --type <TYPE>       Cache type to invalidate. (default: AUTOMATION)
  --yes, -y           Skip confirmation when clearing the entire cache.
  --help, -h          Show this help message.

Global Flags:
  --env <name>        Environment to operate on. (default: nsm-dev)
                      Available: nsm-dev | nsm-qa | nsm-uat
  --llm               Output structured JSON envelope for scripting/LLM use.
  -v, --verbose       Verbose request logging.

Examples:
  # Invalidate three automation cache entries on the active env
  belz cache 44014c3707d6b6d8ae848839a1c8d1e7 47425344e088690ff89aa8f1e092c867 4a1d686b87f93b1519f2b61393d4ed7a

  # Same, against nsm-qa
  belz cache --env nsm-qa <uuid> <uuid>

  # Wipe the entire AUTOMATION cache on nsm-uat (requires confirmation)
  belz cache --env nsm-uat

  # Wipe everything non-interactively (e.g. from a script or LLM)
  belz cache --yes --llm

  # Invalidate by a non-default type
  belz cache --type PAGE <uuid>
